overview: Arunachal Pradesh preserves ceremonial and community dances that reflect tribal identity, seasonal celebration, and storytelling traditions of the eastern Himalayas.

dances.name: Bardo Chham
dances.description: A masked ritual dance performed during monastic festivals with dramatic spiritual symbolism.

dances.name: Ponung
dances.description: A graceful group dance-song tradition of the Adi community led in a circular formation.

dances.name: Popir
dances.description: A women-led folk dance known for elegant hand movements and festive presentation.

dances.name: Aji Lamu
dances.description: A theatrical dance-drama style presented with elaborate costume and expressive movement.

dances.name: Chalo
dances.description: A harvest celebration dance performed in groups to mark joy, unity, and agrarian rhythm.

dances.name: Lion & Peacock Dance
dances.description: A symbolic festival dance that uses animal-inspired costume and movement to create visual storytelling.

overview: Assam's dance culture is energetic and rhythmic, often tied to harvest, devotion, and community celebration across the Brahmaputra valley.

dances.name: Bihu
dances.description: Assam's best-known harvest dance, full of lively steps, drum beats, and youthful celebration.

dances.name: Bagurumba
dances.description: A vibrant Bodo folk dance inspired by the beauty of butterflies and nature.

dances.name: Bhortal Dance
dances.description: A devotional folk form featuring synchronized cymbal playing and dynamic group choreography.

dances.name: Deodhani
dances.description: A dramatic ritual dance associated with spiritual invocation and expressive performance.

dances.name: Jhumura
dances.description: A tea-garden folk dance performed with steady rhythm and a strong collective spirit.

overview: Uttar Pradesh offers folk dances shaped by mythology, village theatre, and festival traditions from the Gangetic plains.

dances.name: Raslila
dances.description: A devotional dance-drama that narrates episodes from the life of Krishna.

dances.name: Nautanki
dances.description: A theatrical folk form blending music, dance, and storytelling in an entertaining village stage style.

dances.name: Charkula
dances.description: A festive Braj dance in which performers balance a decorated multi-tiered lamp arrangement.

dances.name: Mayur Dance
dances.description: A peacock-inspired dance associated with beauty, devotion, and expressive imitation of nature.

dances.name: Karma
dances.description: A traditional group dance performed with circular movement and strong rhythmic repetition.

overview: The folk dances of Uttarakhand often mirror mountain life, warrior heritage, and seasonal celebration in the Himalayan region.

dances.name: Chholiya
dances.description: A sword dance with martial energy, often performed in weddings and ceremonial processions.

dances.name: Langvir Nritya
dances.description: A balancing dance noted for acrobatic control and powerful body strength.

dances.name: Barada Nati
dances.description: A joyful folk performance with coordinated stepping and regional costume tradition.

dances.name: Pandav Nritya
dances.description: A dramatic dance tradition inspired by episodes from the Mahabharata.

overview: Odisha's folk dances combine martial rhythm, devotional expression, and regional performance traditions from coastal and tribal communities.

dances.name: Ghumura
dances.description: A powerful folk dance marked by large drums and vigorous rhythmic movement.

dances.name: Chhau
dances.description: A semi-classical masked dance tradition known for stylized movement and heroic themes.

dances.name: Paika Dance
dances.description: A martial folk dance inspired by the warrior traditions of Odisha.

dances.name: Goti Pua
dances.description: A devotional dance by young performers recognized for flexibility, grace, and expressive poses.

overview: Kerala's folk performances are deeply rooted in temple, festival, and community traditions with expressive group choreography.

dances.name: Kaikottikali
dances.description: A circular women's dance performed with claps, song, and graceful coordinated steps.

dances.name: Kummattikali
dances.description: A festive masked dance associated with colorful costume and public celebration.

dances.name: Thiruvathirakali
dances.description: A refined dance performed by women in a circle during festive and devotional occasions.

dances.name: Ottamthullal
dances.description: A storytelling performance form that mixes expressive acting, dance, and satire.

overview: Karnataka's dance heritage ranges from folk-theatre spectacle to temple and village performance traditions.

dances.name: Yakshagana
dances.description: A celebrated dance-drama tradition with elaborate costume, face paint, and narrative acting.

dances.name: Dollu Kunitha
dances.description: A vigorous drum dance recognized for synchronized footwork and powerful ensemble rhythm.

dances.name: Veeragase
dances.description: A high-energy devotional dance linked to heroic Shaiva traditions.

dances.name: Bhootha Aradhane
dances.description: A ritual performance form with dramatic costume, trance, and ceremonial meaning.

overview: Goa's folk dances reflect coastal village life, festive celebration, and community participation.

dances.name: Fugdi
dances.description: A fast-paced women's folk dance performed in circles with song and rhythmic turns.

dances.name: Dhalo
dances.description: A ceremonial dance-song tradition with paired lines and expressive group movement.

dances.name: Kunbi Dance
dances.description: An agrarian folk dance associated with the Kunbi community and simple rhythmic steps.

overview: Gujarat is famous for lively circular dance traditions celebrated especially during Navratri and community festivals.

dances.name: Garba
dances.description: A colorful circular folk dance performed with claps, spins, and festive devotion.

dances.name: Dandiya Raas
dances.description: A vibrant stick dance marked by rhythmic partner exchange and energetic patterns.

dances.name: Tippani
dances.description: A folk dance traditionally linked with rhythmic labor songs and coordinated stick movement.

overview: Chhattisgarh's folk dances highlight devotion, agrarian celebration, and strong community identity.

dances.name: Panthi
dances.description: A devotional dance performed with vigor, balance, and spiritual focus.

dances.name: Raut Nacha
dances.description: A festive pastoral dance associated with herding communities and celebratory processions.

dances.name: Karma
dances.description: A popular tribal dance featuring circular movement and collective musical rhythm.

dances.name: Sua Nacha
dances.description: A women's folk dance centered on song, clapping, and symbolic storytelling.

overview: Jharkhand's dance traditions are strongly connected to tribal culture, local music, and festive village gatherings.

dances.name: Paika
dances.description: A folk dance with martial roots and sharp rhythmic action.

dances.name: Jhumar
dances.description: A lyrical group dance performed to melodic folk music and swaying movement.

dances.name: Chhau
dances.description: A dramatic dance form blending stylized gesture, athletic movement, and folklore.

overview: Tamil Nadu's folk dances are expressive, community-centered, and often linked to rural festivals and devotional celebration.

dances.name: Karagattam
dances.description: A balancing dance where performers carry decorated pots while moving with grace and control.

dances.name: Kummi
dances.description: A traditional women's folk dance built around clapping, singing, and circular movement.

dances.name: Kolattam
dances.description: A stick dance performed in groups with bright rhythm and patterned choreography.

dances.name: Kavadi
dances.description: A devotional dance linked to pilgrimage and ritual expression.

overview: Telangana's folk dances feature both historic warrior expression and community celebration in vibrant costume.

dances.name: Perini Shivatandavam
dances.description: A vigorous warrior dance associated with masculine energy and rhythmic intensity.

dances.name: Lambadi Dance
dances.description: A colorful community dance of the Lambadi people with expressive costume and lively group movement.

overview: Nagaland's dances often emerge from warrior memory, festival celebration, and strong tribal identity.

dances.name: Chang Lo
dances.description: A victory-themed community dance performed during celebratory gatherings.

dances.name: Zeliang Dance
dances.description: A traditional group dance showing unity, rhythm, and ceremonial pride.

overview: Punjab is known for energetic and festive dance traditions that celebrate harvest, joy, and strong collective spirit.

dances.name: Bhangra
dances.description: A vibrant harvest dance with powerful jumps, shoulder movements, and drum-driven energy.

dances.name: Giddha
dances.description: A lively women's folk dance built around boliyan, claps, and expressive storytelling.

dances.name: Jhumar
dances.description: A graceful Punjabi folk dance known for flowing rhythm and relaxed circular movement.

overview: Bihar preserves dramatic and seasonal folk dances that connect music, storytelling, and village tradition.

dances.name: Jat-Jatin
dances.description: A folk dance-drama based on rural life and emotional exchange between characters.

dances.name: Jhijhiya
dances.description: A festive women's dance involving decorated lamps and rhythmic circular movement.

dances.name: Bidesia
dances.description: A dramatic folk form combining dance, acting, and social storytelling.

overview: The folk dances of Madhya Pradesh reflect community ritual, harvest celebration, and central Indian cultural diversity.

dances.name: Matki
dances.description: A women's dance performed with balancing movement and festive expression.

dances.name: Karma
dances.description: A widely performed tribal folk dance centered on rhythm, community, and celebration.

dances.name: Gangaur Dance
dances.description: A traditional celebratory dance associated with devotion, beauty, and social festivity.

overview: Maharashtra's dance traditions blend performance energy, coastal rhythm, and theatrical folk entertainment.

dances.name: Lavani
dances.description: A powerful performance tradition known for expressive rhythm, song, and dramatic stage presence.

dances.name: Koli Dance
dances.description: A lively dance of the fishing community inspired by rowing and the rhythm of the sea.

dances.name: Tamasha
dances.description: A theatrical folk performance style combining dance, music, and lively entertainment.

overview: Manipur is celebrated for refined and deeply expressive dance traditions rooted in devotion and cultural memory.

dances.name: Ras Leela
dances.description: A graceful devotional dance tradition centered on Krishna legends and lyrical movement.

dances.name: Lai Haraoba
dances.description: A ritual performance celebrating creation stories and sacred cultural heritage.

overview: Mizoram's dance culture is community-oriented and visually striking, often using coordinated patterns and shared rhythm.

dances.name: Cheraw (Bamboo Dance)
dances.description: A famous bamboo dance in which performers move with agility between rhythmically struck poles.

overview: Rajasthan's folk dances are colorful, dramatic, and strongly tied to desert festivals, royal memory, and community celebration.

dances.name: Ghoomar
dances.description: A swirling women's dance recognized for graceful spins and vibrant costume.

dances.name: Kalbelia
dances.description: A serpentine dance tradition of the Kalbelia community with fluid torso and arm movement.

dances.name: Bhavai
dances.description: A balancing dance known for skillful performance with pots and dramatic control.

dances.name: Chari
dances.description: A ceremonial dance in which performers carry decorated brass pots, often with lamps.

overview: Sikkim's dances reflect Himalayan identity, monastic influence, and festive regional culture.

dances.name: Singhi Chham
dances.description: A masked snow-lion dance representing power, purity, and mountain symbolism.

dances.name: Maruni
dances.description: A colorful festive dance performed with musical accompaniment and lively expression.

overview: Haryana's folk dances are energetic and rooted in seasonal celebration, village gathering, and community expression.

dances.name: Phag
dances.description: A spring festival dance celebrating joy, color, and seasonal change.

dances.name: Dhamal
dances.description: A spirited dance marked by strong rhythm and celebratory movement.

dances.name: Khoria
dances.description: A women-led social dance performed during family and wedding occasions.

overview: The folk dances of Himachal Pradesh are shaped by mountain life, festive gatherings, and local ritual traditions.

dances.name: Nati
dances.description: A popular group folk dance with flowing formations and celebratory rhythm.

dances.name: Chamba Dance
dances.description: A regional dance style associated with festive cultural presentation in the Chamba area.

overview: West Bengal's folk traditions combine expressive storytelling, ritual performance, and regional musical culture.

dances.name: Chhau
dances.description: A dynamic masked dance style with heroic movement and strong theatrical energy.

dances.name: Gambhira
dances.description: A folk performance tradition that combines dialogue, rhythm, and social commentary.

dances.name: Santhal Dance
dances.description: A community dance of the Santhal people featuring synchronized group rhythm and song.

overview: Jammu & Kashmir's dance traditions are shaped by valley culture, celebration, and expressive regional costume.

dances.name: Rouf
dances.description: A graceful women's dance performed in rows with gentle rhythmic stepping.

dances.name: Dumhal
dances.description: A ceremonial dance performed by men with colorful costume and rhythmic procession.
